heading: Preemption of State law
label: 77r–1 Preemption of State law
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsecs. (a)(1)(A), (2)(A) and (c), was redesignated by , (c)(1), , .Section 77d(5) of this titlesection 77d(a)(5) of this titlePub. L. 112–106, title II, § 201(b)(1)126 Stat. 314Apr. 5, 2012
CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Secondary Mortgage Market Enhancement Act of 1984, and not as part of the Securities Act of 1933, which comprises this subchapter.

children.heading: Authority to purchase, hold, and invest in securities; securities considered as obligations of United States
children.label: a Authority to purchase, hold, and invest in securities; securities considered as obligations of United States
A
children.text: offered and sold pursuant to section 77d(5) of this title,1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: mortgage related securities (as that term is defined in ),section 78c(a)(41) of this title

children.text: securities issued or guaranteed by the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation or the Federal National Mortgage Association,

children.label: A
children.text: offered and sold pursuant to section 77d(5) of this title,1

children.label: B
children.text: mortgage related securities (as that term is defined in ),section 78c(a)(41) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: small business related securities (as defined in ), orsection 78c(a)(53) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: securities issued or guaranteed by the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation or the Federal National Mortgage Association,

children.heading: Exception; validity of contracts under prior law
children.label: b Exception; validity of contracts under prior law
children.text: The provisions of subsection (a) shall not apply with respect to a particular person, trust, corporation, partnership, association, business trust, or business entity or class thereof in any State that, prior to the expiration of seven years after , enacts a statute that specifically refers to this section and either prohibits or provides for a more limited authority to purchase, hold, or invest in such securities by any person, trust, corporation, partnership, association, business trust, or business entity or class thereof than is provided in subsection (a). The enactment by any State of any statute of the type described in the preceding sentence shall not affect the validity of any contractual commitment to purchase, hold, or invest that was made prior thereto and shall not require the sale or other disposition of any securities acquired prior thereto.October 3, 1984

children.heading: Registration and qualification requirements; exemption; subsequent enactment by State
children.label: c Registration and qualification requirements; exemption; subsequent enactment by State
children.text: Any securities that are offered and sold pursuant to section 77d(5) of this title, that are mortgage related securities (as that term is defined in ), or that are small business related securities (as defined in ) shall be exempt from any law of any State with respect to or requiring registration or qualification of securities or real estate to the same extent as any obligation issued by or guaranteed as to principal and interest by the United States or any agency or instrumentality thereof. Any State may, prior to the expiration of seven years after , enact a statute that specifically refers to this section and requires registration or qualification of any such security on terms that differ from those applicable to any obligation issued by the United States.1section 78c(a)(41) of this titlesection 78c(a)(53) of this titleOctober 3, 1984

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 1 Limitation
children.text: The provisions of subsections (a) and (b) concerning small business related securities shall not apply with respect to a particular person, trust, corporation, partnership, association, business trust, or business entity or class thereof in any State that, prior to the expiration of 7 years after , enacts a statute that specifically refers to this section and either prohibits or provides for a more limited authority to purchase, hold, or invest in such small business related securities by any person, trust, corporation, partnership, association, business trust, or business entity or class thereof than is provided in this section. The enactment by any State of any statute of the type described in the preceding sentence shall not affect the validity of any contractual commitment to purchase, hold, or invest that was made prior to such enactment, and shall not require the sale or other disposition of any small business related securities acquired prior to the date of such enactment.September 23, 1994

children.heading: State registration or qualification requirements
children.label: 2 State registration or qualification requirements
children.text: Any State may, not later than 7 years after , enact a statute that specifically refers to this section and requires registration or qualification of any small business related securities on terms that differ from those applicable to any obligation issued by the United States.September 23, 1994
